# aliBuild

A simple build tool for ALICE experiment software and its externals. Recipes
for the externals and ALICE software are stored in
[alidist](https://github.com/alisw/alidist).

Instant gratification with:

```
pip install alibuild
git clone https://github.com/alisw/alidist.git
aliBuild build AliRoot
alienv enter AliRoot/latest
aliroot -b
```

Full documentation at:

<https://alisw.github.io/alibuild>

# Pre-requisites

If you are using aliBuild directly from git clone, you should make sure
you have `pyyaml` and `argparse` in your python distribution.

classes.name: tests.test_sync.Boto3TestCase

methods.name: tests.test_sync.Boto3TestCase.mock_s3
documentation:
Create a mock object imitating an S3 client.

Which spec we are listing contents for controls the simulated contents
of the store under dist*/:

- MISSING_SPEC: Simulate a case where the store is empty; we can safely
  upload objects to the remote.
- GOOD_SPEC: Simulate a case where we can fetch tarballs from the store;
  we mustn't upload as that would overwrite existing packages.
- BAD_SPEC: Simulate a case where we must abort our upload.

This currently only affects the simulated contents of dist*
directories.

methods.name: tests.test_sync.Boto3TestCase.test_tarball_download
documentation: Test boto3 behaviour when downloading tarballs from the remote.

methods.name: tests.test_sync.Boto3TestCase.test_tarball_upload
documentation: Test boto3 behaviour when building packages for upload locally.
Check the b3:// remote is working properly.

methods.name: tests.test_deps.DepsTestCase.test_deps
documentation: Check doDeps doesn't raise an exception.

classes.name: tests.test_hashing.KnownGoodHashesTestCase
documentation:
Make sure storeHashes produces the same hashes as in a build log.

It is assumed that the hashes in the build log are correct, i.e. the ones
we want to get for the matching spec in the log.

It is possible to provide old-style logs (mentioning one local and remote
hash only) or new-style logs (mentioning all alternative remote and local
hashes). If providing old-style logs, only the hashing for the primary
hashes is checked.

classes.name: alibuild_helpers.sl.Sapling
documentation: Sapling is a novel SCM by Meta (i.e. Facebook) that is fully compatible with
git, but has a different command line interface. Among the reasons why it's
worth suporting it is the ability to handle unnamed branches, the ability to
absorb changes to the correct commit without having to explicitly rebase and
the integration with github to allow for pull requests to be created from the
command line from each commit of a branch.

modules.name: alibuild_helpers.analytics
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.analytics.decideAnalytics
documentation: Helper function to decide whether or not we should run analytics.
It's done this way so that we can easily test all the alternatives.
This is the rationale to enable the analytics:
- In case user disabled analytics via environment variable or by
answering no when prompted the first time. Just run as usual.
- In case there is already an analytics user id, it means the user
already replied yes to the question wether he wants analytics or
not, so we proceed with analytics.
- If we are not running in a tty, run without analytics.
- In case there is no analytics id, ask wether is ok to have
analytics. If no, remember the answer and disable it. If yes,
generate a uuid with uuidgen and remember it.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.analytics.disable_analytics
documentation: We do it in getstatusoutput because python makedirs can actually fail
if one of the intermediate directories is not writeable.

modules.name: alibuild_helpers.deps

Single-source a PEP440-compliant version using setuptools_scm.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.args.alidist_string
documentation: This is syntactic sugar for the --dist option (which should really be called
--dist-tag). It can be either:
- A tag name
- A repository spec in the for org/repo@tag

modules.name: alibuild_helpers.clean
documentation: Import as function if they do not have any side effects

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.clean.decideClean
documentation:
Decide what to delete, without actually doing it.

To clean up obsolete build directories:
- Find all the symlinks in "BUILD"
- Find all the directories in "BUILD"
- Schedule a directory for deletion if it does not have a symlink

Installed packages are deleted from the final installation directory
according to the above scheme as well.

The temporary directory and temporary install roots are always cleaned up.

In aggressive mode, the following are also cleaned up:

- Tarballs (but not their symlinks), since these are expected to either be
  unpacked in the installation directory, available from the remote store
  for download, or not needed any more if their installation directory is
  gone.
- Git checkouts for specific tags, since we expect to be able to rebuild
  those easily from the mirror directory.

In the case of installed packages and tarballs, only those for the given
architecture are considered for deletion.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.clean.doClean
documentation: CLI API to cleanup build area

don't change, don't track in version control

modules.name: alibuild_helpers.utilities
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.utilities.symlink
documentation:
Match the behaviour of `ln -nsf LINK_TARGET LINK_NAME`, without having to fork.

Create a new symlink named LINK_NAME pointing to LINK_TARGET. If LINK_NAME
is a directory, create a symlink named basename(LINK_TARGET) inside it.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.utilities.topological_sort
documentation:
Topologically sort specs so that dependencies come before the packages that depend on them.

This function returns a generator, yielding package names in order.

The algorithm used here was adapted from:
http://www.stoimen.com/blog/2012/10/01/computer-algorithms-topological-sort-of-a-graph/

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.utilities.resolve_store_path
documentation:
Return the path where a tarball with the given hash is to be stored.

The returned path is relative to the working directory (normally sw/) or the
root of the remote store.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.utilities.resolve_links_path
documentation:
Return the path where symlinks for the given package are to be stored.

The returned path is relative to the working directory (normally sw/) or the
root of the remote store.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.utilities.short_commit_hash
documentation:
Shorten the spec's commit hash to make it more human-readable.

This is complicated by the fact that the commit_hash property is not
necessarily a commit hash, but might be a tag name. If it is a tag name,
return it as-is, else assume it is actually a commit hash and shorten it.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.utilities.resolve_version
documentation:
Expand the version replacing the following keywords:

- %(commit_hash)s
- %(short_hash)s
- %(tag)s
- %(branch_basename)s
- %(branch_stream)s
- %(tag_basename)s
- %(defaults_upper)s
- %(year)s
- %(month)s
- %(day)s
- %(hour)s

with the calculated content.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.utilities.resolve_tag
documentation:
Expand the tag, replacing the following keywords:
- %(year)s
- %(month)s
- %(day)s
- %(hour)s

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.utilities.validateDefaults
documentation: Use this to check if a given spec is compatible with the given default

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.utilities.detectArch
documentation: Try to guess a good platform. This does not try to cover all the
possibly compatible linux distributions, but tries to get right the
common one, obvious one. If you use a Unknownbuntu which is compatible
with Ubuntu 15.10 you will still have to give an explicit platform
string.
FIXME: we should have a fallback for lsb_release, since platform.dist
is going away.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.utilities.getRecipeReader
documentation: Get the appropriate recipe reader depending on th filename

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.utilities.parseDefaults

Override defaultsGetter for unit tests.

classes.name: alibuild_helpers.utilities.GitReader

classes.name: alibuild_helpers.scm.SCMError
documentation: Signal that an SCM-related error occurred.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.cmd.decode_with_fallback
documentation:
Try to decode DATA as utf-8; if that doesn't work, fall back to latin-1.

This combination should cover every possible byte string, as latin-1 covers
every possible single byte.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.cmd.getoutput
documentation: Run command, check it succeeded, and return its stdout as a string.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.cmd.getstatusoutput
documentation: Run command and return its return code and output (stdout and stderr).

classes.name: alibuild_helpers.cmd.DockerRunner
documentation:
A context manager for running commands inside a Docker container.

If the Docker image given is None or empty, the commands are run on the host
instead.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.build.update_git_repos
documentation:
Update and/or fetch required git repositories in parallel.

If any repository fails to be fetched, then it is retried, while allowing the
user to input their credentials if required.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.build.createDistLinks
documentation: Creates a directory in the store which contains symlinks to the package
and its direct / indirect dependencies

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.build.storeHashes
documentation:
Calculate various hashes for package, and store them in specs[package].

Assumes that all dependencies of the package already have a definitive hash.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.build.hash_local_changes
documentation:
Produce a hash of all local changes in the given git repo.

If there are untracked files, this function returns a unique hash to force a
rebuild, and logs a warning, as we cannot detect changes to those files.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.build.better_tarball
documentation: Return which tarball we should prefer to reuse.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.build.generate_initdotsh
documentation:
Return the contents of the given package's etc/profile/init.sh as a string.

If post_build is true, also generate variables pointing to the package
itself; else, only generate variables pointing at it dependencies.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.build.create_provenance_info
documentation: Return a metadata record for storage in the package's install directory.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.workarea.cleanup_git_log
documentation:
Remove a stale fetch-log.txt.

You must call this function before running updateReferenceRepoSpec or
updateReferenceRepo any number of times. This is not done automatically, so
that running those functions in parallel works properly.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.workarea.logged_scm
documentation:
Run an SCM command, but produce an output file if it fails.

This is useful in CI, so that we can pick up SCM failures and show them in
the final produced log. For this reason, the file we write in this function
must not contain any secrets. We only output the SCM command we ran, its exit
code, and the package name, so this should be safe.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.workarea.updateReferenceRepoSpec
alibuild_helpers.workarea.updateReferenceRepo
alibuild_helpers.workarea.checkout_sources
documentation: Check out sources to be compiled, potentially from a given reference.

modules.name: alibuild_helpers.templating_plugin
documentation:
A text templating plugin for aliBuild.

This plugin allows reusing specs like those that would be used during the
build, for instance to get the "real" version numbers for various packages.

We use Jinja2 as the templating language, read the user-provided template from
stdin and print the rendered output to stdout.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.templating_plugin.build_plugin
documentation: Read a user-provided template from stdin and render it.

modules.name: alibuild_helpers.sync
documentation: Sync backends for alibuild.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.sync.remote_from_url
documentation: Parse remote store URLs and return the correct RemoteSync instance for them.

classes.name: alibuild_helpers.sync.NoRemoteSync
documentation: Helper class which does not do anything to sync

classes.name: alibuild_helpers.sync.RsyncRemoteSync
documentation: Helper class to sync package build directory using RSync.

classes.name: alibuild_helpers.sync.CVMFSRemoteSync
documentation:
Sync packages build directory from CVMFS or similar
FS based deployment. The tarball will be created on the fly with a single
symlink to the remote store in it, so that unpacking really
means unpacking the symlink to the wanted package.

classes.name: alibuild_helpers.sync.S3RemoteSync
documentation:
Sync package build directory from and to S3 using s3cmd.

s3cmd must be installed separately in order for this to work.

classes.name: alibuild_helpers.sync.Boto3RemoteSync

methods.name: alibuild_helpers.sync.Boto3RemoteSync._s3_listdir
documentation: List keys of items under dirname in the read bucket.

methods.name: alibuild_helpers.sync.Boto3RemoteSync._s3_key_exists
documentation: Return whether the given key exists in the write bucket already.

Sync package build directory from and to S3 using boto3.

As boto3 doesn't support Python 2, this class can only be used under Python
3. boto3 is only imported at __init__ time, so if this class is never
instantiated, boto3 doesn't have to be installed.

This class has the advantage over S3RemoteSync that it uses the same
connection to S3 every time, while s3cmd must establish a new connection each
time.

functions.name: alibuild_helpers.git.clone_speedup_options
documentation: Return a list of options supported by the system git which speed up cloning.

variables.name: alibuild_helpers.git.GIT_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_SEC
documentation: Default value for how many seconds to let any git command execute before being terminated.

variables.name: alibuild_helpers.git.GIT_CMD_TIMEOUTS
